methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Climate & Energy. Range: $110M - $1.8B.

data.narrative:
Cylib, a Series A climate and energy company founded by Lilian Schwich, Gideon Schwich, and Paul Sabarny, has raised €140M+ to date, comprising a €55M Series A tranche plus €26.1M in NRW grant funding and €63.4M in BMWE grant funding. The valuation analysis draws on a peer set of four same-sector, same-stage private companies: Watershed ($1.8B), Graphyte ($200M), Fourth Power ($150M), and Steady Energy ($110M).

The peer-median method applies a median valuation across the four comparable companies, yielding $175M. This cohort exhibits substantial dispersion, with valuations ranging from $110M to $1.8B, reflecting heterogeneity in technology maturity and market positioning within the Climate & Energy frontier-tech segment. The median of $175M sits materially above the distribution's lower quartile, reflecting Cylib's substantial capitalization relative to early-stage peers in the sector.

The triangulated valuation range derived from applicable methods is $149M to $201M, with a central estimate of $175M. This analysis is constrained by reliance on a single methodological approach; incorporation of revenue multiples, discounted cash flow assumptions, or comparable transaction data would strengthen conviction. Key uncertainties include the sustainability of grant funding, commercialization trajectory relative to peer benchmarks, and technology differentiation within an increasingly crowded climate-tech landscape.
